description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Hanno City Hall has launched a social experiment in partnership with the Oku-Musashi Tourism Association. Starting today, April 1st, through June 30th (Reiwa 6), food trucks and market stalls will operate on weekday lunch hours at the city hall grounds.

This initiative aims to make better use of public facilities as a resource, improve convenience for residents, create new business opportunities for local vendors, and support sustainable city management.

Food trucks operate almost daily, and market stalls are held once a week on the main office building's wood deck.

Different vendors rotate in each day, and the public is welcome to visit. Please stop by!

For details about this project and vendor information, see the URL below:

http://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/sogoseisakubu/shisankeieika/13132.html

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/shiseijoho/shichoshitsu/shichoblog/shisei2026/shisei202604/13450.html
summary: 📝 Mayor's Report: Starting a Social Experiment with Food Trucks and Market Stalls
